title: Iran disputes claims of new agreements with Trump
description: Tehran put limits on the Strait of Hormuz’s opening, as the United States issued a new sanctions exemption on the sale of Russian oil.

title: Trump plans to ease access to psychedelics like psilocybin, ibogaine
description: The president will direct federal agencies to boost research into the drugs and support clinical trials, moves championed by Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

title: Trump came to Arizona to rally young voters. An older crowd greeted him.
description: Despite some empty seats, Trump projected confidence in his party’s ability to win the midterm elections. Some in the crowd focused on GOP divisions.

title: Agencies in talks with Trump, family to resolve $10B lawsuit over tax leaks
description: President Trump and his family are suing federal agencies over leaks of their tax information. Court filings say they’re in talks to find a solution instead of heading to trial.

title: In Las Vegas, Trump’s economic pitch meets a skeptical audience
description: Trump touts “no tax on tips” in Las Vegas as voters grapple with high prices and economic unease.

title: House Democrats push to grill Stephen Miller, Tom Homan
description: Democrats want to grill the top immigration officials in a hearing before the House Homeland Security Committee. But the White House did not say if they’ll participate.

title: Supreme Court hands win to Chevron, Big Oil in environmental damage case
description: The decision puts into question a $745 million judgment against Chevron to help restore coastal wetlands in Louisiana that were damaged as long ago as World War II.

title: Congress approves 10-day extension for warrantless surveillance law
description: Trump officials say the program is vital to national security, but skeptics — including some Republicans — have stonewalled its reauthorization without changes to protect civil liberties.

title: Trump administration deepens quest to stamp out the events of Jan. 6
description: In his second term, the president has doubled down on efforts to reframe public perception of the 2021 attack on the U.S. Capitol, in court and beyond.

title: Logjam of U.S. immigration applications puts millions at greater risk of deportation
description: An NPR analysis shows how immigrants' attempts to live or work legally in the U.S. are caught in a bureaucratic morass.

title: White House seeks record defense budget, but Congress has questions about spending
description: The White House is seeking $1.5 trillion from Congress for the Pentagon and is expected to send a separate ask for the Iran war. Lawmakers want more transparency around how the money will be spent.

title: Mejia wins NJ special election to replace Gov. Sherrill in Congress
description: Democrat Analilia Mejía won a special election Thursday to fill the House seat vacated by Democratic Gov. Mikie Sherrill.

title: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani on Trump and the future of the Democratic Party
description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Mayor Zohran Mamdani about New York City, President Trump and the future of the Democratic Party.

title: Trump rails against court decision that once again stalls his White House ballroom project
description: The federal judge's decision continues to block above-ground construction on the $400 million White House ballroom, allowing only below-ground work on a bunker and other "national security facilities" at the site.

title: Congress extends controversial surveillance powers for 10 days
description: Earlier in the morning GOP leaders had pushed for either a five-year renewal or the 18-month renewal President Trump had demanded, but both votes tanked.

title: Former FEMA leader set to return after being ousted
description: Cameron Hamilton had publicly disagreed with previous efforts to dismantle FEMA. While those plans have shifted, the agency’s future is still unclear.

title: Acting ICE director says he's resigning amid record-breaking shutdown
description: The departure adds to a string of recent personnel changes at the Department of Homeland Security, a year into President Trump's administration.

title: 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins as Israel agrees to U.S.-backed deal
description: President Donald Trump announced the agreement, which went into force Thursday evening, as Pakistani mediators worked to extend a U.S.-Iran ceasefire and arrange new talks.

title: Trump says Iran agrees to hand over ‘nuclear dust’
description: Iran has not confirmed Trump’s claim. Giving up its highly enriched uranium would be a major step toward an agreement.

title: Trump officially taps Erica Schwartz to lead CDC, avoiding vaccine skeptics
description: A new team could mark a shift away from Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s unpopular vaccine agenda ahead of the midterms.

title: Republicans in Congress back Army secretary amid clash with Hegseth
description: Dan Driscoll, the top political appointee in the Army, gave his first public testimony since Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth forced out the service’s top officer.

title: House passes bill extending protections for Haitian migrants in the U.S.
description: Ten Republicans voted alongside Democrats, in a rebuke to the Trump administration's immigration policies. Should it pass the Senate, the White House said President Trump would veto the measure.

title: Federal judge sets new limits on Trump ballroom construction
description: U.S. District Judge Richard Leon clarified that some national security work can continue while the overall project is halted. Trump attacked him on social media.

title: House votes to restore protections for Haitians, defying Trump
description: Ten House Republicans joined Democrats to oppose President Donald Trump on his immigration policy Thursday, voting to restore temporary protections for Haitians living in the United States.

title: Top five takeaways from Homeland Security budget hearings
description: Lawmakers have been in a stalemate for over 60 days about funding the entire department, which includes agencies that oversee immigration enforcement, disaster relief, cybersecurity and the U.S. Coast Guard.

title: Voters in Virginia could have one of the final words in Trump's redistricting fight
description: Virginians will decide whether the state will redraw its congressional voting map. A win would give Democrats an edge in four more seats, meaning they could hold 10 of Virginia's 11 seats in Congress.

title: House narrowly rejects resolution directing Trump to end ‘hostilities’ in Iran
description: The Senate turned back a similar measure Wednesday seeking to rein in the president as the unpopular war approaches the two-month mark.

title: How every House member voted on blocking Trump from striking Iran
description: The House is voting on a measure to block President Trump from ordering more strikes on Iran. See how your lawmaker voted.

title: Trump’s 250-foot arch receives early design approval as public pans it
description: The Commission of Fine Arts asked the architect for revisions, with one member suggesting the project could be downsized by leaving out gold-plated statues.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: Largely secret donors pour millions into election that could tip Congress
description: The vast majority of the nearly $100 million raised to sway Virginia’s redistricting battle, mostly on the Democratic side, consists of untraceable dark money.

title: Trump’s top arts commissioner says D.C. needs three arches, not one
description: Rodney Mims Cook Jr. tried to build an arch in D.C. for years. Now his panel will review the president’s own proposal — and Cook says two more should be built, too.

title: Trump tests loyalty of Christian supporters as erratic behavior escalates
description: The president maintains his hold on the Christian right, but his bluster, fight with the pope and portrayal of himself as a Jesus-like figure strain that bond.
